prompt: Tell me a bio of Adil Rami
evaluation_criteria: Factual accuracy of nationality (French), profession (footballer), clubs played for, international career with France, position (centre-back), birth date, and career milestones. Club transfer dates, caps for national team, and specific tournament appearances must be verified.
output: Adil Rami (born 27 December 1985 in Bastia, Corsica) is a French former professional footballer of Moroccan descent who played as a centre-back. His remarkable career took him from amateur football at Fréjus (fourth division) — where he worked odd jobs for the city to support himself — to winning the 2018 FIFA World Cup with France. He played for Lille (2006–2011, winning a league-cup double), Valencia (2011–2014), AC Milan (2014–2015), Sevilla (2015–2017), Marseille (2017–2019), Fenerbahçe, Sochi, Boavista, and Troyes (2021–2023). He earned 36 caps for France (2010–2018), was a Euro 2016 runner-up, and was part of the 2018 World Cup-winning squad. Known by his nickname "Shrek," Rami's story is one of football's most extraordinary underdog tales. In 2025, he appeared on French reality TV shows including Danse avec les stars (finishing 3rd).
